MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Chu from Brayton & Purcell (415-898-1555) for plaintiffs/opposing parties Burt Howard and Cheryl Howard.

Ruling:
No other appearance and tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Duro Dyne Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Howard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement demonstrated only that Mr. Howard has no personal knowledge whether any of the Duro Dyne flexible duct connectors with which he worked contained asbestos. That does not sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs will be unable to prove their case by any means. Defendant's separate statement did not properly raise the sufficiency of plaintiffs' discovery responses on the topic of asbestos content. Even if it had, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories would preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee; not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brian S. O'Malley of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (925-947-1300), appearing for defendant / moving party KB Home San Diego, Inc., erroneously named herein as KB Home Holdings, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Burt Howard and Cheryl Howard

RULING:
DEFENDANT KB HOME SAN DIEGO, INC., ERRONEOUSLY NAMED HEREIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant KB Home San Diego, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the basis for its motion warrants summary judgment. Defendant argues that plaintiffs cannot support a claim for premises/contractor liability pursuant to Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and its progeny. However even assuming that plaintiffs cannot support such a claim, defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs' Negligence II cause of action fails as plaintiffs' claims against defendant are based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees rather than the theories of liability articulated in Privette and the premises liability line of cases. Assuming that defendant properly raised the issue of whether there is evidence that defendant's employees exposed Mr. Howard to asbestos-containing products or materials, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support that contention. Mr. Howard's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel J. Lee of ROSS WERSCHING & WOLCOTT LLP (714-444-3900), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / moving party Cambridge Financial Management, Inc.
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Burt Howard and Cheryl Howard

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAMBRIDGE FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cambridge Financial Management, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled in its entirety. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit A and denied as to Exhibit B. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment to complaint fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action based on the time period identified. If the Court granted defendant's request for judicial notice of Exhibit B, the Court would only take judicial notice of the existence of the Business Entity Detail, not the truth of the facts stated within. Finally, even if the Court took judicial notice of the fact that Cambridge Financial Management, Inc. filed in 1983, it still would not demonstrate that plaintiffs fail to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against defendant. Defendant's demurrer on the ground of uncertainty is improper and/or not sufficiently specific. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' allegations are insufficient pursuant to Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' claims are barred by Workers' Compensation exclusivity. Based on the above, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff's loss of consortium cause of action fails. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Desiree Winn of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13645 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
